title: 12th Mental Health Leadership & Advocacy Course in Egypt 2026: Fully Funded Opportunities for African Public Health Leaders
summary: The growing urgency of addressing mental health challenges across Africa has led to the creation of transformative leadership initiatives. One of the most impactful among them is the 12th Mental Health Leadership Advocacy Course , organized under the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) in collaboration with The American University in Cairo . Set to take place from 10–22 May 2026 in Cairo , Egypt , this prestigious program is designed to equip emerging leaders with the skills, knowledge, and networks needed to transform mental health systems across the African continent. Why This Course Matters for Africa’s Mental Health Future Mental health remains one of the most under-addressed public health challenges in Africa. Limited resources, stigma, and policy gaps continue to hinder progress. The MHLP directly tackles these issues by preparing a new generation of leaders capable of influencing: National mental health policies Legislative reforms Community-based interventions Health system strengthening Through a public health and human rights lens , participants gain a comprehensive understanding of how to design and implement impactful mental health strategies. About the Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) The Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) is a flagship three-year initiative by Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention . It focuses on building leadership capacity across Africa’s health sector by: Promoting innovative approaches to mental health care Encouraging cross-sector collaboration Supporting advocacy at local, national, and continental levels Strengthening evidence-based policymaking Participants in this course become part of a powerful network of professionals working to reshape mental health systems across Africa. What You Will Gain from the Course This intensive two-week program offers a unique combination of academic learning, practical experience, and networking opportunities. Key Benefits: Advanced training in mental health leadership and advocacy Exposure to global and African best practices Hands-on learning through case studies and group work Direct engagement with experts and policymakers Membership in a pan-African network of changemakers Participants will leave with the tools needed to design impactful mental health initiatives and advocate for systemic change. Scholarships and Funding Opportunities One of the most attractive aspects of this course is the availability of fully and partially funded opportunities . Fully Funded Scholarships The MHLP by Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention will sponsor: 25 public health professionals from African Union Member States Coverage includes participation in the course in Cairo Partial Scholarships The American University in Cairo offers limited scholarships for applicants from low-income countries in the Middle East Course Fees for Self-Funded Participants For those who wish to self-sponsor, the course remains accessible with tiered pricing: Residents of Egypt: $500 Low-income countries (World Bank classification): $800 Middle-income countries: $1000 High-income countries: $1500 Note: Fees include course materials but exclude accommodation and meals. How to Apply Interested applicants must complete the official registration form and clearly indicate their preferred funding option: Africa CDC Scholarship AUC Scholarship Self-funded participation Applicants are encouraged to highlight their experience, motivation, and any innovative mental health projects they are involved in. Application Deadline The final deadline to apply is 5 April 2026 . Late applications are unlikely to be considered due to the competitive nature of the program. Final Thoughts The 12th Mental Health Leadership Advocacy Course is more than just a training—it is a career-defining opportunity for individuals committed to transforming mental health systems in Africa. Whether you are a public health professional, a mental health advocate, or someone with lived experience, this program provides a platform to amplify your impact and lead meaningful change . If you are passionate about shaping the future of mental health in Africa, this is your chance to step forward and lead. APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post 12th Mental Health Leadership Advocacy Course in Egypt 2026: Fully Funded Opportunities for African Public Health Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Africa CDC & AVMI Industrial Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded Vaccine Manufacturing Training for African Graduates
summary: The Africa CDC and African Vaccine Manufacturing Initiative (AVMI) Industrial Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is now open for applications, offering a highly competitive and fully funded opportunity for recent African graduates to gain hands-on experience in vaccine manufacturing. This flagship programme is designed to address Africa’s urgent need for skilled professionals in vaccine research, development, and manufacturing. With the continent aiming to significantly scale up its vaccine production capacity by 2040, this fellowship plays a critical role in building a strong, self-sufficient workforce. Background and Rationale Africa currently faces a significant gap in vaccine manufacturing capacity, both in terms of infrastructure and skilled human resources. According to projections, the continent will need approximately 12,500 full-time professionals in vaccine manufacturing by 2040. However, the current workforce ranges between only 2,000 and 3,000 individuals, many of whom are not fully specialized in vaccine production. Traditional reliance on international training programmes has proven to be costly and unsustainable due to financial constraints and visa-related challenges. Additionally, there is a shortage of industrial placements within Africa to provide graduates with practical, hands-on experience. To bridge this gap, the Africa CDC, in collaboration with AVMI, launched this structured industrial fellowship programme to train and equip the next generation of vaccine manufacturing professionals. About the Industrial Fellowship Programme The fellowship is a one-year intensive training programme focused on developing competencies aligned with the Vaccine Research and Development and Manufacturing Competency Frameworks established by Africa CDC. Fellows will be placed in active vaccine manufacturing institutions across selected African countries, where they will gain practical exposure to industry processes and standards. Key Features of the Programme Duration: July 2026 to September 2027 Placement Locations: Egypt, Senegal, or South Africa Focus Area: Vaccine manufacturing and current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) Cohort Size: Only 14 fellows will be selected What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows will receive comprehensive financial and professional support, including: 1. Financial Support Return flight tickets to the host country Visa fees and required documentation support A stipend to cover basic daily expenses 2. Hands-On Industry Experience Practical training in vaccine manufacturing facilities Exposure to real-world pharmaceutical production processes Experience aligned with global manufacturing standards (cGMP) 3. Career Development Development of technical and industrial competencies Increased employability in vaccine manufacturing institutions Opportunity to contribute to Africa’s public health advancement Fields of Study Eligible Applicants must have a background in one of the following disciplines: Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences Pharmaceutical Biotechnology Chemistry or Biochemistry Chemical Engineering Biomanufacturing or Vaccine Manufacturing Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this fellowship, applicants must: Be a citizen of an African Union Member State Be a recent graduate (within the last 3 years) with a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree Possess a valid passport Be available to participate in the programme in one of the designated countries Be proficient in English or French The programme is inclusive and encourages applications from all qualified individuals regardless of gender, race, or background. Women are strongly encouraged to apply. Selection Process Applications will be reviewed by a selection committee comprising Africa CDC, PHAHM, and AVMI representatives Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted A total of 14 fellows will be selected for the programme Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 Notification of Selected Candidates: May 15, 2026 Programme Duration: July 2026 – September 2027 Applicants are strongly advised to apply early to increase their chances of selection. How to Apply Interested candidates must complete the official application form via the link below: Apply for the Africa CDC Industrial Fellowship Programme Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a rare opportunity to gain specialized, industry-level experience in vaccine manufacturing within Africa. Participants will: Acquire critical technical skills in a high-demand field Contribute to strengthening Africa’s health security systems Enhance their career prospects in biotechnology and pharmaceuticals Join a select group of future leaders in vaccine manufacturing Learn more about the Africa CDC and African Vaccine Manufacturing Initiative (AVMI) Industrial Fellowship Programme HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: Gender and Security Fellowship 2026–2027 at Princeton University: Research, Policy, and Leadership Opportunity for Students
summary: The Gender and Security Fellowship 2026–2027 , hosted by the Liechtenstein Institute on Self-Determination at Princeton University, is a prestigious academic and professional development opportunity designed for students interested in the intersection of gender, governance, and global security. This fellowship brings together a highly competitive cohort of undergraduate and graduate students to engage in cutting-edge research, policy development, and collaborative programming. Participants gain hands-on experience contributing to meaningful policy discussions and advancing inclusive perspectives in global affairs. About the Gender and Security Fellowship The fellowship is part of the Gender in the Global Community project , led by Dr. Barbara Buckinx, and coordinated by Sydney Leigh Smith (PhD, Security Studies). It serves as a dynamic platform for students to explore critical global issues through interdisciplinary research and applied policy work. Fellows are not only researchers but also active contributors to academic and policy-oriented programming, including reading groups and discussions that shape contemporary debates on gender and security. What the Fellowship Offers Participants in the program will benefit from a wide range of academic, professional, and networking opportunities: 1. Research Policy Engagement Contribute to policy-relevant outputs such as: Policy briefs Analytical reports Academic publications Work on real-world issues related to gender, governance, and global security 2. Skill Development Strengthen research and analytical capabilities Develop methodological and critical thinking skills Gain experience in interdisciplinary approaches to global challenges 3. Leadership Collaboration Participate in designing and facilitating the Gender and Security Reading Group Engage in collaborative discussions and programming Build leadership skills through active academic involvement 4. Networking Opportunities Connect with peers, researchers, and experts in the field Become part of a scholarly and policy-focused community at Princeton University Program Focus Areas The fellowship emphasizes key global themes, including: Gender and global governance Conflict and security studies Intersectionality and inclusive policymaking International development and political systems Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Gender and Security Fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be a current undergraduate or graduate student at Princeton University Demonstrate strong interest in gender, governance, and security-related issues Show academic excellence and commitment to research and policy engagement Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 Interview Period: Week of April 13, 2026 Final Decision Notification: April 17, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and ensure all requirements are met before submission. Why You Should Apply The Gender and Security Fellowship provides a unique opportunity to: Engage directly with policy-relevant research Build a strong academic and professional portfolio Gain exposure to global governance and security issues Develop leadership and facilitation skills Join a network of future leaders and scholars This fellowship is particularly valuable for students pursuing careers in international relations, public policy, security studies, gender studies, and related fields. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the Official Fellowship Platform before the deadline. For further inquiries, applicants may contact the fellowship coordinator via email: sydney.s@princeton.edu . Click HERE for more information about the Gender and Security Fellowship 2026–2027. For more global Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY HERE. title: Apply for the NDC Youth Ambassador Program Spring 2026: Paid Climate Policy Fellowship for Youth
summary: The NDC Youth Ambassador Program Spring 2026, organized by Care About Climate (CAC), is a unique global initiative designed to empower young people to actively participate in climate policy analysis and advocacy. This program provides a structured pathway for youth to engage with one of the most critical frameworks guiding global climate action: Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs). By combining training, practical experience, and a focus on inclusive perspectives, the program seeks to amplify youth voices in shaping climate solutions at the national level. About Care About Climate (CAC) Care About Climate (CAC) is a non-profit organization committed to breaking barriers and creating meaningful pathways for young people to engage in climate policy spaces. The organization focuses on equipping youth with the knowledge, tools, and opportunities needed to influence climate decision-making processes. Through initiatives like the NDC Youth Ambassador Program, CAC continues to foster a new generation of informed and active climate leaders. Understanding NDCs and Their Global Importance As part of the Paris Agreement, a landmark global accord on climate change, countries are required to submit Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs). These documents outline each country’s commitments and strategies to mitigate greenhouse gas emissions, adapt to climate impacts, and mobilize climate finance. NDCs are central to global climate action, as they reflect national priorities and ambitions. However, despite their importance, many NDCs are often reviewed without adequately considering youth perspectives or gender-inclusive approaches. This creates a significant gap in how climate policies are analyzed and implemented. Bridging the Gap: A Youth and Feminist Perspective The NDC Youth Ambassador Program addresses this gap by introducing an intergenerational and gender lens to NDC analysis. The program recognizes that climate change disproportionately affects young people and marginalized groups, yet their voices are frequently underrepresented in policy discussions. By engaging youth directly in reviewing their country’s NDCs, the program ensures that these perspectives are not only included but also amplified. This approach promotes more equitable, inclusive, and effective climate policies. Program Overview and Structure Selected participants will serve as NDC Ambassadors and commit approximately five weeks to the program. During this period, ambassadors will focus on reviewing and analyzing their own country’s NDC. This country-specific approach is designed to strengthen local engagement and ensure authenticity in the analysis. Participants will receive structured training on how to evaluate NDCs through a gender and intergenerational lens. This training is essential for equipping ambassadors with the analytical frameworks and tools needed to conduct meaningful assessments. In addition to the training, each ambassador will receive a stipend of 100 USD as compensation for their time and contributions. Eligibility and Country Representation The program is open to applicants from a select group of countries. Interested individuals must be from or currently living in the country they wish to represent. This requirement ensures that participants have a direct connection to the context they are analyzing. The eligible countries for the Spring 2026 cohort include: Dominican Republic Liberia European Union Colombia Turkey Switzerland Mexico Thailand Indonesia Malaysia Mali Democratic Republic of the Congo Vanuatu Costa Rica Australia El Salvador Ukraine The Philippines China Tuvalu Republic of Korea Nepal Program Timeline and Commitment The NDC Youth Ambassador Program Spring 2026 will run from April 6 through May 11, 2026. Within this timeframe, participants are expected to: Attend all required training sessions Conduct a comprehensive analysis of their country’s NDC Submit their completed work by the program deadline The program requires a consistent commitment throughout the five-week period, making it important for applicants to ensure their availability before applying. Application Deadline and Process All applications must be submitted by 5:00 PM GMT on April 1, 2026. APPLY HERE Late submissions will not be considered, so prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their materials well in advance of the deadline. Skills and Background Requirements While prior experience in climate action or knowledge of climate politics can be beneficial, it is not a requirement for participation. The program is designed to be accessible to a wide range of young people, including those who are new to climate policy. Importantly, the program emphasizes authenticity and integrity in the application process. The use of artificial intelligence tools in application submissions is strictly prohibited, and any applications found to contain AI-generated content will be disqualified. Benefits of Participation Participating in the NDC Youth Ambassador Program offers several valuable benefits: Hands-on experience in climate policy analysis Training in gender-responsive and intergenerational frameworks Opportunity to contribute to global climate discourse Financial support through a 100 USD stipend Networking with a global community of young climate advocates Contact and Additional Resources For any questions or further information about the program, interested applicants can reach out via email at Ruth@careaboutclimate.org . To explore existing work and learn more about NDC analysis, visit NDCEquityTracker.org, a platform dedicated to tracking and evaluating climate commitments through an equity-focused lens. Conclusion The NDC Youth Ambassador Program Spring 2026 stands out as a powerful initiative for youth engagement in climate governance. By combining education, practical experience, and a strong emphasis on inclusivity, the program equips young people to critically assess and influence national climate commitments. For young individuals passionate about climate change and eager to make a tangible impact, this program represents a significant opportunity to contribute to shaping a more equitable and sustainable future. Discover more similar opportunities here The post Apply for the NDC Youth Ambassador Program Spring 2026: Paid Climate Policy Fellowship for Youth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 🌍 Fully Funded PhD in Human Rights, Global Politics & Sustainability (Italy, 2026–27)
summary: The Scuola Superiore Sant’Anna has officially opened applications for its fully funded PhD in Human Rights, Global Politics and Sustainability for the 2026–2027 academic year. Hosted in Pisa , this prestigious doctoral programme is designed for candidates aiming to conduct high-impact interdisciplinary research across Political Science, International Law, and Political Philosophy. Programme Overview Location: Pisa, Italy Duration: 3 years (full-time, residential) Funding: Fully funded positions only Research Areas: Human Rights Global Politics Sustainability This programme encourages applicants to submit innovative research proposals that combine at least two disciplines , fostering a truly interdisciplinary academic experience. Funding, Benefits and Support Selected candidates will receive a generous funding package that includes: Annual scholarship of €19,740 (gross) Housing allowance of €2,200 (gross) Approximate net monthly stipend of €1,600 26 free meals per month 50% increase in funding for research abroad Personal research budget (10% of scholarship) Students also gain access to academic facilities, including libraries, seminars, and research support services throughout the programme. Programme Structure and Academic Experience The PhD programme is carefully structured over three years: Year 1 Mandatory coursework Training in research methodologies and soft skills Core interdisciplinary courses in: Human Rights Global Politics Sustainability Year 2 Mandatory international research period (minimum 6 months abroad) Year 3 Thesis writing and submission After submission, the thesis is evaluated by external referees, followed by a viva examination within a defined timeframe. The programme is fully residential , requiring students to live in Pisa and actively participate in academic life. Eligibility Criteria To apply for this PhD programme, candidates must meet the following requirements: Hold a Master’s degree or equivalent qualification Candidates completing their degree by September 30, 2026 are also eligible to apply Degrees from disciplines outside the core fields are accepted if applicants demonstrate strong relevant knowledge English proficiency is required, as the programme is fully taught in English Students will also be expected to learn an additional foreign language (minimum A2 level) during the programme. Important Notes The programme is fully funded only ; self-funded applications are not accepted It is a full-time commitment , and students cannot engage in other employment Accommodation is not provided, but students receive a housing allowance No special arrangements are provided for students with families Required Application Documents Mandatory Valid ID Academic transcripts and certificates Curriculum Vitae Motivation letter Research proposal (in English) Incomplete applications will not be considered. Optional Academic publications Up to 3 recommendation letters MSc thesis or abstract Application Timeline Application Deadline: May 15, 2026 (12:00 p.m. CEST) Interview Schedule: To be announced Final Results: By July 15, 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered. Discover more internship opportunities, click here. Why You Should Apply This PhD programme offers a rare opportunity to: Conduct interdisciplinary research on global challenges Study in one of Italy’s leading institutions Gain international exposure and research experience Build a career in academia, policy, or international organizations With strong funding, structured training, and a global focus, this programme is ideal for ambitious scholars ready to make a meaningful impact. title: Girls Who Code Pathways 2026: Free Virtual Summer Program to Learn AI, Coding & Build Tech for Good
summary: Introduction The Girls Who Code Pathways 2026 Summer Program is a free, flexible, and fully virtual learning experience designed to empower high school girls and non-binary students to explore technology and create meaningful, purpose-driven projects. Over seven weeks, participants gain hands-on experience in some of the most in-demand fields in tech—all while learning at their own pace. Whether you re a beginner or already have coding experience, Pathways offers a supportive community, real-world exposure, and the opportunity to build skills that matter. Program Overview Program Name: Girls Who Code Pathways Format: 100% Virtual, Self-Paced Duration: 7 Weeks Dates: June 29, 2026 – August 14, 2026 Cost: Free This asynchronous program allows students to complete coursework on their own schedule while still benefiting from optional live events, mentorship opportunities, and peer collaboration. Application Deadlines Extended Early Deadline: February 27, 2026 Final Deadline: April 10, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure their spot and access all program benefits. Why You Should Apply Participants in the Pathways program will: Learn in-demand tech skills such as AI, cybersecurity, and web development Build real-world, portfolio-ready projects Earn certificates for completed courses Connect with industry professionals and mentors Join a global community of peers through Discord and virtual events Gain insights into tech careers and emerging technologies Available Learning Tracks Students can explore multiple tracks based on their interests: 1. Data Science + AI (Python) Learn data analysis, visualization, and machine learning basics Work with decision trees and neural networks Focus on responsible and ethical AI 2. Cybersecurity (Python) Understand digital security and cryptography Build projects like chatbots and encryption tools Explore the social impact of cybersecurity 3. Web Development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) Build interactive websites Create a portfolio, quiz, and advocacy site Learn front-end development fundamentals 4. Game Design (JavaScript p5.js) Design interactive games and storytelling experiences Learn prototyping, UX design, and playtesting Create your own playable projects 5. AI + Society (No Coding Required) Understand how AI impacts society Learn prompt engineering and ethical AI use Create AI-related projects and guides Program Structure Experience Each course includes step-by-step tutorials and a hands-on project. Students typically spend: 3–6 hours per course 8–15 hours per full track Participants can complete multiple courses and earn certificates for each. To become a program alum, students must complete at least one course. Additional opportunities include: Live Advisor Partner Events Weekly Student Hours for support Networking via a private Discord community Who Can Apply? High school girls and non-binary students (grades 8–12 in 2025–2026 school year) International students aged 14–18 are also eligible Students of all coding levels , including beginners Returning students and alumni from previous Girls Who Code programs are also welcome to apply. How to Apply The application takes about 10 minutes to complete Must be submitted by the student (not a parent or teacher) Cannot be edited after submission Students under 18 should review with a parent/guardian APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more summer courses opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The Girls Who Code Pathways Program is more than just a coding course—it’s a gateway to future careers in tech, a chance to build impactful projects, and an opportunity to join a global network of changemakers. If you re ready to explore technology, gain real-world skills, and build something meaningful this summer, don’t miss this opportunity. title: Newmark J+ Executive Program 2026–2027: Transform Your Leadership in News Innovation
summary: The future of journalism demands bold, adaptive, and visionary leadership. As the media industry navigates rapid technological change, shifting business models, and evolving audience expectations, the need for innovative leaders has never been greater. The Newmark J+ Executive Program in News Innovation and Leadership at the Craig Newmark Graduate School of Journalism at CUNY is designed to equip experienced media professionals with the tools, strategies, and networks needed to lead transformation in the news industry. Program Overview This prestigious year-long executive program brings together a carefully selected cohort of media leaders from across the globe. Participants gain practical insights into leadership, innovation, and business strategy while continuing to work in their current roles. Running from August 2026 through June 2027 , the hybrid format combines online learning with in-person residencies in New York City. Why This Program Stands Out The program goes beyond traditional journalism training by focusing on the intersection of: Editorial leadership Product innovation Business strategy Technology integration Participants are trained to develop sustainable revenue models, lead organizational change, and build forward-thinking media products. Key Benefits Participants of the program will: Strengthen leadership and decision-making skills Learn to navigate the evolving media business landscape Develop innovative revenue and growth strategies Build data-driven, audience-first approaches Gain hands-on experience through a capstone project Receive personalized coaching from industry experts Join a global network of media innovators and executives Graduates often advance into senior leadership roles, secure promotions, or launch new ventures within the media space. Program Structure The Executive Program is designed for working professionals and includes: Three in-person residencies in New York City August/September 2026 (Program Start) February 2027 (Midpoint) June 2027 (Final Residency Graduation) Weekly Online Sessions 2-hour classes every Tuesday Monthly leadership sessions on Thursdays Capstone Project Apply your learning to a real-world leadership or innovation challenge Executive Coaching Personalized guidance throughout the program Curriculum Highlights Participants will explore critical areas such as: The Business of Journalism Product Management Development Revenue Models Business Strategy Managing Technology Innovation Audience Data Metrics Leadership in the Age of AI Organizational Culture Change Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Responses to leadership-focused questions Two letters of recommendation Updated resume A letter of intent (up to 1,000 words) Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview with the admissions team. Tuition and Financial Support Program Cost: $27,000 Includes tuition, coaching, materials, and networking events Scholarships: Limited full and partial need-based scholarships are available Deadline Applications for the 2026–2027 cohort are open until May 1, 2026 . How to Apply Interested candidates can apply through the official program page or contact the admissions team via email at leadership@journalism.cuny.edu for further inquiries. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts In a time when journalism faces unprecedented challenges, this program offers a unique opportunity to step into leadership with clarity, confidence, and purpose. By joining this executive cohort, you position yourself at the forefront of innovation in the global media landscape. title: FAO Global Conference on Smart Farming 2026: Driving the Future of Sustainable Agrifood Systems
summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) is set to host the Global Conference on Smart Farming: Leveraging Data and Technology for Sustainable Agrifood Systems from July 1–3, 2026 . This highly anticipated global event will take place in Rome , Italy , with both in-person and virtual participation options available. About the Conference The FAO Global Conference on Smart Farming brings together a diverse community of stakeholders—including policymakers, researchers, innovators, youth leaders, and practitioners—to explore how technology can transform agrifood systems. The conference focuses on harnessing data, digital tools, and innovation to create more sustainable, resilient, and inclusive food systems worldwide. Key Focus Areas Participants will engage in discussions around critical themes shaping the future of agriculture: Scaling smart farming systems for small-scale producers Empowering youth and women entrepreneurs in agrifood Leveraging data, analytics, and emerging technologies Strengthening sustainability and climate resilience Driving collaboration across sectors for long-term impact Why You Should Attend This conference offers a unique platform to: Connect with global experts and stakeholders Learn about cutting-edge innovations in agrifood systems Explore opportunities for collaboration and investment Contribute to shaping policies and strategies for sustainable agriculture Whether you are a policymaker, entrepreneur, researcher, or development practitioner, this event provides valuable insights and networking opportunities. Conference Details Dates: July 1–3, 2026 Location: Rome, Italy (Hybrid format – in-person and virtual) How to Participate To learn more about the conference and participation details, visit here. title: Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 in AI and Climate-Responsive Urban Design at University of Groningen
summary: The Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 at the University of Groningen offers a unique and forward-thinking research position for individuals passionate about youth participation, climate-responsive cities, and the ethical integration of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in urban design. Hosted by the Faculty of Spatial Sciences, this fully funded PhD project explores how AI can transform participatory urban planning through inclusive and interdisciplinary approaches. With applications open until 30 April 2026 (23:59 Dutch local time), this opportunity is ideal for candidates seeking to contribute to cutting-edge research that bridges technology, society, and sustainable urban development. Innovative Research Focus on AI and Participatory Urban Design This PhD project centers on a groundbreaking concept known as “Vibe-Designing”, a co-design methodology that integrates Generative AI into participatory urban design processes. The approach positions AI tools as mediators that facilitate interactive, visually rich dialogue between youth, urban planners, designers, and policymakers. The research aims to address a critical gap: while youth and marginalized communities are increasingly recognized as essential stakeholders in urban development, their participation in decision-making processes remains limited. At the same time, Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how urban spaces are imagined and designed. This project seeks to combine these two dynamics in a meaningful and ethical way. Innovative Objectives of the AI and Climate-Responsive Urban Design PhD Project The research will investigate several key dimensions, including: • How Generative AI reshapes participatory practices and power dynamics in urban planning • The role of cultural, institutional, and governance contexts in adopting AI-mediated design approaches • How youth engage with AI tools to influence climate-responsive urban futures By combining theoretical insights with empirical research, the project aims to develop practical frameworks that can be applied in real-world urban planning contexts. Innovative Responsibilities of the PhD Candidate The selected candidate will undertake a range of academic and practical responsibilities, culminating in a PhD thesis composed of four high-quality journal articles. Key responsibilities include: • Conducting a systematic literature review on AI in participatory planning • Carrying out qualitative and ethnographic fieldwork in urban environments • Engaging with policymakers, practitioners, AI developers, and community stakeholders • Designing and facilitating co-design workshops involving youth and AI tools • Analyzing ethical, cultural, and institutional dimensions of AI integration • Disseminating research findings through publications and conferences • Contributing to teaching activities (approximately 15% of time) The project adopts a multi-method research approach, including interviews, focus groups, participant observation, and visual-based methodologies. Innovative Academic Environment at the University of Groningen The University of Groningen is one of Europe’s leading research institutions, known for its interdisciplinary approach and strong commitment to addressing global challenges. The Faculty of Spatial Sciences plays a critical role in advancing research on sustainability, urban development, and societal transformation. With a vibrant academic community of over 100 PhD students and international researchers, the faculty provides an environment that fosters innovation, collaboration, and impactful research. The university emphasizes inclusivity, diversity, and a safe working environment, ensuring that all researchers can thrive and contribute meaningfully. Innovative Benefits and Funding Package This PhD position offers a competitive and comprehensive funding package in line with Dutch university standards. Key benefits include: • Monthly salary ranging from €3,059 to €3,881 • Full-time employment for four years (subject to performance evaluation after the first year) • 232 vacation hours annually (based on a 38-hour workweek) • Additional holiday allowance (8%) and end-of-year bonus (8.3%) • Opportunities for professional and personal development • Support for research dissemination and international engagement The position provides not only financial stability but also a strong platform for academic and professional growth. Innovative Candidate Profile The program seeks highly motivated and intellectually curious individuals with a strong academic background and interest in interdisciplinary research. Ideal candidates should have: • A Master’s degree in urban planning, human geography, urban studies, anthropology, or related fields • Experience with qualitative research methods such as ethnography, interviews, or focus groups • Strong communication skills and cultural sensitivity • Excellent command of English Additional experience in participatory design, digital tools, or AI ethics is considered an advantage, though advanced technical AI skills are not required. Deadline for Applications The deadline to apply for this Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 is 30 April 2026 (23:59 Dutch local time). Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 in AI and Urban Design and apply, click here : APPLY HERE Conclusion The Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 at the University of Groningen offers a rare chance to contribute to transformative research at the intersection of technology, urban planning, and social inclusion. By exploring how Generative AI can empower youth participation in climate-responsive cities, this program positions candidates at the forefront of future urban innovation. title: Google PhD Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Doctoral Researchers
summary: The Google PhD Fellowship Program is now open for applications, offering exceptional doctoral students the opportunity to receive funding, mentorship, and global recognition for their research. This prestigious fellowship is designed to support PhD students conducting innovative research in computer science and related fields, helping them advance their academic careers while connecting with world-class experts. About the Google PhD Fellowship Program The Google PhD Fellowship recognizes outstanding graduate students who are making significant contributions in research areas aligned with Google’s focus. Beyond financial support, the fellowship provides access to mentorship from leading researchers, creating a strong bridge between academia and industry. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows benefit from a robust support package, including: Full or partial funding covering tuition and living expenses Mentorship from a Google Research Mentor Global recognition as a Google PhD Fellow Access to a network of top researchers and professionals Opportunities for career growth and collaboration Important Application Note Applications cannot be submitted directly by students . All applications must be submitted through an official university representative during the application window. This means interested candidates should coordinate with their department or institution early to ensure timely submission. Why You Should Apply The Google PhD Fellowship is one of the most competitive and prestigious fellowships globally. It not only provides financial relief but also opens doors to collaboration, visibility, and future career opportunities in both academia and industry. How to Apply Learn more and start your application process here. For more Fellowship Opportunites, Visit OFY Here.
